texto: You're right, Ruth. You did screw up my career. You screwed it up good. Best thing you ever did, matter of fact. But you know something? It's okay. Because if it was between the church and you, there was no contest. Even with al the ups and downs and the craziness and the shit and the maxed-out credit cards, the church never stood a chance. I chose you, Ruth. And I'm glad. There. That's what my so-called career was about. And that's what I should have said to you. And I'm sorry I didn't. I'm sorry, Ruth. I'm sorry.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de K. D. Miller que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “You're right, Ruth. You did screw up my career. You screwed it up good. Best thing you ever did, …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, church, marriage, com foco em casamento e marriage.
